explanation: This LTL property ensures that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will transition from the TRANSITION state to the NOMINAL state in the next time step. This is captured by the implication in the LTL property, where the antecedent represents the condition (supported system and good sensor data), and the consequent represents the desired outcome (transitioning to the NOMINAL state in the next time step).

This LTL property ensures that the autopilot transitions from the TRANSITION state to the NOMINAL state whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good. The property uses a combination of implication and the next operator to express this requirement. The implication  (supported & good) -> (X state_is_NOMINAL)  states that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the next state must be NOMINAL. The G operator ensures that this implication holds globally, meaning it must hold in all states. Overall, this LTL property ensures that the autopilot behaves as intended by transitioning to the NOMINAL state when the necessary conditions are met.

This LTL property states that whenever the system is supported and the sensor data is good, the autopilot will transition to the NOMINAL state in the next time step.  It is captured using a "whenever condition1 is true, then condition2 is true in the next time step" template. The condition1 here is "supported" and the condition2 is "(state_is_NOMINAL & !state_is_TRANSITION)".

The LTL property states that whenever the system is supported (supported = True) and sensor data is good (good = True),  the autopilot will change states from TRANSITION to NOMINAL in the next time step. This captures the natural language input by ensuring that the autopilot transitions to the NOMINAL state only when the required conditions are met, and it does so in the next time step.

This LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot must transition from the TRANSITION state to the NOMINAL state in the next time step. It uses the 'G' operator to ensure that this implication holds globally (at all times) and the 'X' operator to specify that the transition to the NOMINAL state should occur in the next time step. The boolean expressions 'supported' and 'good' represent the atomic propositions indicating system health and sensor data validity, respectively. The boolean expression "state_is_TRANSITION" represents the current state being TRANSITION, and "state_is_NOMINAL" represents the next state being NOMINAL.

This LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot should transition from the TRANSITION state to the NOMINAL state.  This is achieved by using the implication operator:  'G (supported & good -> X (state_is_NOMINAL & (! state_is_TRANSITION)))'.  This property states that globally (for all time steps), if the system is supported ('supported') and sensor data is good ('good'), then in the next time step, the autopilot will be in the NOMINAL state ('state_is_NOMINAL') and not in the TRANSITION state ('! state_is_TRANSITION').  This ensures that the autopilot changes states as required.

This LTL formula ensures that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good (bool_exp2), the autopilot will eventually change to the NOMINAL state (bool_exp3) within 2 time steps. It uses template 14 and sets N_DURATION to 2.

This LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will transition from the TRANSITION state to the NOMINAL state in the next time step.  The boolean expression bool_exp2 is used to represent the condition of system supported and sensor data being good, while bool_exp3 is used to represent the autopilot transitioning to the NOMINAL state.  The LTL template ensures that whenever bool_exp2 is true, bool_exp3 will be true in the next time step.

It uses template 11 to express this transition requirement,  ensuring that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good (bool_exp2), in the next time step (X) the autopilot will be in the NOMINAL state (bool_exp3).

The property uses the atomic proposition "state_is_NOMINAL" to express the autopilot's state, "supported" for the system's health, and "good" for the sensor data. The LTL operator "X" is used to indicate the next time step.

This LTL property ensures that the autopilot transitions from the "TRANSITION" state to the "NOMINAL" state only when the system is supported ("supported" is true) and sensor data is good ("good" is true). The property uses the "G" (globally) operator to enforce this condition at all times.  The boolean expression within the LTL formula uses the implication operator to check that if both "supported" and "good" are true, then the autopilot is in the "NOMINAL" state (using the atomic proposition "state_is_NOMINAL") in the next time step.

The LTL formula captures the requirement that whenever the system is supported and the sensor data is good, the autopilot will transition from the TRANSITION state to the NOMINAL state in the next time step. It uses the implication operator (->) to express the relationship between the conditions (supported and good) and the consequence (transitioning to NOMINAL in the next state). The atomic propositions "supported", "good", "state_is_TRANSITION", and "state_is_NOMINAL" represent the system's state and sensor data.

The consequent of the implication states that eventually, the autopilot will be in the NOMINAL state ('state_is_NOMINAL').

The property uses the "F" operator to state that the autopilot will eventually transition to the NOMINAL state.

The LTL formula captures the requirement that when the system is supported (supported=True) and sensor data is good (good=True), the autopilot shall eventually transition from TRANSITION state (state_is_TRANSITION=True) to NOMINAL state (state_is_NOMINAL=True). It uses the "eventually" (F) operator to indicate that the transition should happen at some point in the future, and the "and" (&) operator to combine the conditions for supported and good sensor data. The formula also ensures that the transition occurs within a maximum of N_DURATION time steps (N_DURATION=2).

The right-hand side of the implication represents the consequence: in the next time step, the autopilot will be in the NOMINAL state (X state_is_NOMINAL).
